System.name: Legacy IT Student Portal
System.type: Web application (SPA frontend + REST API backend)
System.architecture: Modular monolith — React SPA + Node.js/Express API + PostgreSQL, integrated with the university OSAS API for student identity, profiles, and schedules
System.target_users: IT Department students, faculty, and administrators

Objectives.primary: Digitize and secure class attendance through rotating QR codes with real-time monitoring
secondary: Centralize student, faculty, subject, and section records
Provide role-specific dashboards and accurate exportable reports
Reduce attendance fraud (proxy scanning, screenshot sharing) via token rotation and server-side validation
Lay a modular foundation so future portal modules (grades, clearance, etc.) plug in cleanly
success_metrics: Attendance recorded in under 5 seconds per student
Zero duplicate attendance records (enforced by DB constraint)
Reports generated in under 10 seconds for a full term

User Roles.Super Admin: IT system owner. Full access to all modules, settings, users, and audit logs.
User Roles.Department Admin: Manages department data: students, faculty, subjects, sections, offerings, schedules; approves corrections; runs reports.
User Roles.Faculty: Runs class sessions, displays QR codes, monitors live attendance, overrides statuses with reasons, exports own class reports.
User Roles.Student: Scans QR to log attendance, views own records and summaries, files correction requests, receives notifications.
User Roles.Guest: Unauthenticated visitor. Sees only the public landing page and login.

students.method: Single sign-on with university credentials via the OSAS API — no separate registration; accounts already exist in OSAS
students.verification: Credentials and student status checked against the university database through the OSAS API at every login
students.eligibility: Current, active students only — alumni and inactive students are rejected (OSAS status check before granting access)
students.profile_sync: student_id, program, year, section, status, and profile picture pulled/refreshed from OSAS on login

faculty_admin.method: Local email + password accounts provisioned by admins
faculty_admin.first_login: Force password change on provisioned accounts
faculty_admin.optional_2fa: Email OTP for Super Admin / Department Admin

Authentication.session_strategy: JWT access token (15 min, memory) + rotating refresh token (7 days, httpOnly Secure cookie) for all roles once verified
Authentication.lockout: 5 failed attempts → 15-minute lock; logged to login_logs
Authentication.device_tracking: Each refresh token records device/user-agent info; sessions are listable and revocable per device
Authentication.logout: Refresh token revoked server-side; cookie cleared

Navigation.layout: Collapsible sidebar (desktop) / bottom-sheet drawer (mobile) + topbar with search, notifications bell, theme toggle, and profile menu

Modules.Authentication: Login, logout, refresh, password reset, account lockout, optional OTP
Modules.Student Management: CRUD, bulk CSV import, photo upload, status lifecycle, section assignment
Modules.Faculty Management: CRUD, employment info, subject assignment view
Modules.Subject Management: CRUD subjects (code, title, units, type lecture/lab), curriculum tagging
Modules.Section Management: CRUD sections per course/year, capacity, adviser assignment
Modules.QR Attendance: Session lifecycle (start/close), dynamic QR regenerating every minute (static option for events), student scanner, server-side validation, duplicate prevention
Modules.OSAS Integration: Connects to the university OSAS API: student SSO/eligibility checks, profile sync (ID, program, year, section, status, photo), weekly schedule import, and calendar exceptions (holidays, suspensions, makeup classes, special events)
Modules.Attendance Monitoring: Live roster during session, real-time present/late counts, historical browsing, manual override, correction workflow
Modules.Reports: Per-session sheets, per-student history, summaries, at-risk lists; PDF/XLSX/CSV export
Modules.Notifications: In-app real-time (Socket.IO) + email (Nodemailer); per-user read state and preferences
Modules.Settings: Academic term, late/absence thresholds, QR rotation interval, email templates, module toggles

qr_attendance: Faculty starts a session → fullscreen dynamic QR (regenerates every 1 minute) for projector display; one QR per class or event
QR payload is encrypted and time-limited; screenshots expire within the minute and fail server validation
Static QR option for specific events/classes (admin-enabled, reduced security)
Student scans with phone camera (html5-qrcode) → instant confirmation with status (Present/Late)
Scan retries allowed within a short time frame for failed reads; only the first valid scan is recorded (duplicate prevention)
No GPS or classroom location verification — the dynamic QR is the presence proof
Server-authoritative timestamps; client clock never trusted
Auto-close session at scheduled cutoff time (configurable); manual attendance by faculty for students with technical issues
Auto-mark unscanned enrolled students as Absent on close
monitoring: Live roster updates via WebSocket as scans arrive
Session summary snapshot saved on close
corrections: Student files request with reason and optional attachment
Faculty/Admin approves or rejects; every change audited
quality_of_life: Dark/light theme, keyboard-accessible UI, WCAG AA contrast
Bulk CSV import with validation preview
PWA install for quick student access to the scanner

Business Rules.BR-01: A student can have exactly one attendance record per class session (DB unique constraint on session_id + student_id).
Business Rules.BR-02: Scans are only valid while the session is open and the rotating token is within its validity window (60s, ±1 window).
Business Rules.BR-03: Scan within grace period (default 15 min from session start) = Present; after grace period = Late; after cutoff (default 30 min) or never = Absent.
Business Rules.BR-04: Only enrolled students of the offering can record attendance for its sessions.
Business Rules.BR-05: Manual overrides require a reason and are written to the audit log with before/after values.
Business Rules.BR-06: Excused status can only be set via approved correction or by faculty/admin, never by scanning.
Business Rules.BR-07: 3 consecutive absences or ≥20% total absences triggers an at-risk notification to the student and adviser.
Business Rules.BR-08: Deactivated/dropped students keep historical records but cannot log new attendance.
Business Rules.BR-09: Faculty can only manage sessions for offerings assigned to them for the active term.
Business Rules.BR-10: All destructive admin actions use soft delete; hard delete is Super Admin only and audited.
Business Rules.BR-11: Late/grace thresholds are configurable per subject offering, falling back to system defaults.
Business Rules.BR-12: Reports always compute from raw attendance_records at request time (no stale denormalized truth).
Business Rules.BR-13: Student login requires an active/current status in OSAS; alumni and inactive students are rejected at authentication time.
Business Rules.BR-14: No attendance can be submitted after the session cutoff time (except staff manual entries, which are audited).
Business Rules.BR-15: Sessions are not generated on holidays or suspension dates from the calendar exceptions; makeup classes and special events generate extra sessions.
Business Rules.BR-16: Static QR mode is per-session, requires admin enablement, and is flagged in reports as reduced-security.

sessions: GET /sessions/:id/qr-token (current rotating token — faculty only, polled/pushed)
API Endpoints.realtime: Socket.IO namespace /live — rooms: session:{id} (scan events), user:{id} (notifications)

Validation Rules.engine: zod schemas shared conceptually between frontend forms (react-hook-form resolver) and backend middleware

examples.email: Valid format, lowercase, max 254 chars, unique on create
examples.password: Min 8, at least 1 upper, 1 lower, 1 number
examples.schedule: end_time > start_time; no overlapping schedules for the same section, faculty, or room
examples.scan_payload: sessionId uuid + token string; everything else server-derived
examples.csv_import: Row-level validation with per-row error report before commit (all-or-nothing transaction optional)

general: All input validated at the API boundary; unknown keys stripped
IDs validated as UUIDs; pagination capped (max 100 per page)

Security.headers: helmet defaults + strict CSP for the SPA
Security.auth_tokens: Short-lived access JWT; rotating refresh tokens hashed at rest; revocation on logout/password change
Security.rbac: Middleware role checks + resource-ownership checks on every protected route
Security.rate_limiting: Global 300 req/15min per IP; /auth/login 10 req/15min; /attendance/scan 10 req/min per user
Security.input_safety: Prisma parameterized queries (no raw SQL), zod validation, output encoding in React (XSS-safe by default)
Security.csrf: SameSite=Lax cookies + custom header requirement on state-changing requests
Security.qr_fraud: Encrypted (AES-256-GCM), time-limited rotating QR payloads (60s regeneration), single-use per student per session, server timestamps — screenshots and replays fail validation
Security.device_tracking: Refresh tokens store device/user-agent metadata; users and admins can view and revoke sessions per device
Security.osas_api: Server-to-server only (credentials never reach the browser); API keys in env vars; responses validated with zod before persistence; sync failures logged and alerted
Security.uploads: MIME + magic-byte checks, randomized filenames, served from separate path/domain
Security.audit: All auth events, overrides, deletions, settings changes, and correction reviews logged with actor, IP, before/after
Security.secrets: .env per environment, never committed; example file .env.example tracked

Reports.session_sheet: Roster with status, scan time, and totals for one session — printable PDF
Reports.student_history: Chronological records + per-subject summary and overall rate for a term
Reports.offering_summary: Per-student rates in a class, session count, Present/Late/Absent/Excused totals
Reports.at_risk: Students crossing absence thresholds, with counts and last-seen date
Reports.department_overview: Rates by course/year/section, trends by week

exports.async: Large exports generated server-side and delivered via notification link

admin: KPI tiles (today's rate, active sessions, total students/faculty)
Dashboard.implementation: Single GET /dashboard endpoint returning role-shaped payload; widgets lazy-rendered; charts via Recharts

Notifications.in_app: Socket.IO push to user:{id} room; bell dropdown with unread count; persisted in notifications table
Notifications.email: Nodemailer + SMTP (provider-agnostic); templated with MJML-compiled HTML; used for account, reset, and at-risk alerts
Notifications.preferences: Per-user toggles per event type (in-app always on for critical events)

attendance: Allow static QR for events (false by default)
Settings.storage: Key-value (jsonb) settings table with typed accessors and cache

Deployment.containers: Docker Compose: nginx (reverse proxy + static SPA) | api (Node 20 alpine) | postgres 16 | optional redis (socket.io adapter when scaling)
Deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions: lint → typecheck → test → build → deploy on main; migrations run via prisma migrate deploy

environments.production: HTTPS (Let's Encrypt), daily DB backups, health checks

Deployment.monitoring: pino logs → file/aggregator; /healthz endpoint; uptime monitor
Deployment.config: 12-factor via environment variables; .env.example documents all keys

technical: PWA offline scan queue (sync on reconnect)
Read replicas and Redis cache if load grows
Future Plans.design_principle: Every module ships behind a feature flag in settings; new modules follow the same feature-folder pattern on both frontend and backend
